meta.title: Shinbo Family Ski Area
meta.description: ファミリー特化・西日本

hero.title:
Shinbo Family
Ski Area
hero.tagline: ファミリー特化・西日本

parking.label: Parking
parking.note: 5 min by car from Kamikawa Station

sanctuary.title:
Free lifts,
resort-grade snow.
sanctuary.lead: Inland cold at the foot of the Daisetsu range brings top-tier dry powder. While lift prices rise nationwide, skiing here is completely free—with meticulous grooming that earns reviews like "too good to be free." A hidden town-run gem.
sanctuary.noticeBody: Chairlift retired; one rope tow only. Snowboards allowed everywhere. Closed Mondays (except holidays). Season and hours vary with snow conditions.
sanctuary.imgAlt: Dry powder on a wide gentle slope under the Daisetsu range—a free town-run hill

primary.title: Spotless lodge & restrooms
primary.lead: A renovated base lodge and restrooms praised in reviews—the infrastructure that matters most at small local hills. A decisive differentiator that extends stay time for families, women, and overseas guests.
primary.imgAlt: Renovated clean ski lodge—a new bar for local hills

secondary.title: Complement to Kurodake
secondary.lead: Kurodake in the same town targets advanced skiers. Nakayama is the safe first-snow base for beginners, families, and Sounkyo onsen guests when steep alpine terrain is too much.

freemium.title:
Free skiing,
paid experiences.
freemium.lead: Keep free lifts for locals while monetizing overseas snow-play rental packs (tubes, sleds, wear), guided powder tours, and digital donations—a freemium model that turns external visitors into revenue.
freemium.imgAlt: Snow-play rental at the lodge—tubes and sleds for hands-free fun

wellness.title:
After skiing,
Kamikawa ramen.
wellness.lead: Kamikawa town brands itself "Japan's best ramen town"—authentic bowls minutes from the hill. Then Kamikawa Daiginjo brewery for tasting and premium souvenirs—the core of area circulation.
wellness.leadStation: Lodge café and coworking on weekdays—capturing workation demand too.
wellness.imgAlt: Steaming Kamikawa ramen—local food after skiing

tour.title:
From Sounkyo,
snow & culture.
tour.lead: A half- to full-day model for Sounkyo onsen guests. Position Nakayama as a weather-resilient first-snow base when Kurodake is too steep—maximize town spending on food, sake, and night skiing.

am.title: Nakayama Ski Area
am.body: Snow play & beginner lessons from the clean base lodge

noon.title: Kamikawa ramen
noon.body: Lunch in town. Multilingual ramen map

eve.title: Night skiing (optional)
eve.body: Until 21:00 Tue·Thu·Sat. Shuttle from onsen town

powderTour.title: Guided premium powder tour
powderTour.body: Hike-up and powder runs in the former upper lift zone with local guides (¥15,000/person half day). Flip "no chairlift" into "untouched powder"—high-margin content for core skiers and affluent overseas guests.

snowPlay.title: Overseas snow-play rental pack
snowPlay.body: Hands-free tubes, sleds, and snow wear (¥3,000/2h). Posters in English and Chinese at Sounkyo hotels—direct cash from visitors who can't use the rope tow.

donation.title: Digital donation & supporter program
donation.body: Multilingual QR payment panels for tips (¥500–1,000) to maintain the hill. Supporter stickers for ¥1,000+ donations—channeling "too good to be free" gratitude into revenue.

access.note: Asahikawa Station ~1 hr by car · Kamikawa-Sounkyo IC ~15 min · JR Kamikawa Station taxi ~10 min, walk ~20 min · 100 free parking spaces

access.rentacarLink: Book a rental car at Memanbetsu Airport
access.rentacarHint: For Nemuro, Shibetsu, and Abashiri drives
